lesson.title: Beat the rotation with the cross-court pass.
lesson.body_md:
## What is Skip the Rotation?

A paint touch makes the defense slide. By the time the next pass arrives, the closest defender is the one who *just rotated*. The Skip pass — across the court — beats them to the spot, because the rotation never gets there.

```takeaway
When the defense is rotating, throw the ball to the spot they can't get to in time.
```

## The cue

- A teammate's drive forces help to commit.
- Two defenders are rotating across the court at the same time.
- The opposite corner or wing is open *before* the closeout starts.

```tip
If the help rotated, the skip is on. Don't wait for the rotation to finish.
```

## How to punish it

1. **See the second rotation, not just the first.** Help on the drive is one rotation; the next defender filling for them is the second.
2. **Throw it on a line.** A skip pass that floats lets the closeout catch up.
3. **Trust the receiver.** The catch is the read; don't pre-decide whether they shoot, drive, or skip again.

```mistake
Forcing the skip when only one defender has rotated. The closest perimeter defender is still in position — they'll deflect or strip it.
Fix: wait for the second rotation, then throw.
```

## Self-check after the rep

```reveal
Q: Did I see the help commit before I skipped?
A: Skip without rotation is a turnover. Read the help first.
```

```reveal
Q: Did I throw it on a line, not a rainbow?
A: A flat skip beats the closeout. A floater lets the defense recover.
```

```reveal
Q: Did I trust my teammate to make the next read?
A: Your job ends when the ball arrives. The catch is theirs.
```

## Try it

```quiz
Q: A teammate's paint touch pulled help. The opposite corner is open before any defender has closed out. Best move?
- Drive again ✗
- Pass back to the slot ✗
- Skip pass to the open corner ✓
Why: The skip beats the rotation. The corner shooter sees the closeout long before it arrives.
```

```coach
The pass beats the run. Skip it.
```
